perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that it would be impossible or inadequate to measure and calculate the other party's damages from any breach of the covenants set forth in this Agreement. Accordingly, the parties agree that if either party breaches or threatens to breach any of such covenants, the non-breaching party will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach and to specific performance of any such provision of this Agreement. The parties further agree that no bond or other security shall be required in obtaining such equitable relief and each party hereby consents to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges that damages from a breach of this agreement may be difficult to quantify. The parties agree to attempt mediation before seeking legal remedies.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows the non-breaching party to seek an injunction or specific performance in case of a breach. By changing it to 'The parties agree to attempt mediation before seeking legal remedies,' the modified version removes the right to seek immediate equitable relief (injunction, specific performance) and introduces a mandatory mediation step. This directly contradicts the original clause, which provided immediate access to injunctive relief, and creates uncertainty by adding a step that could delay or prevent enforcement. Mediation is non-binding, so it undermines the original enforcement rights by requiring it before any legal action can be taken.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges that damages from a breach of this agreement may be difficult to quantify. The parties agree to attempt mediation before seeking legal remedies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the right to seek immediate equitable relief (injunction, specific performance) with a mandatory mediation step. This directly contradicts the original clause, which provided immediate access to injunctive relief. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, confirming that the change contradicts the earlier version of the clause.
